narration: Science reveals the hidden truth about the incubation period.

narration: The incubation period is the time between exposure to a pathogen and the onset of symptoms.
visual_description: Context visual: The incubation period is the time between exposure to a pathogen and the onset of symptoms.
ai_image_prompt: Context visual: The incubation period is the time between exposure to a pathogen and the onset of symptoms.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Context visual: The incubation period is the time between exposure to a pathogen and the onset of symptoms.

narration: This can vary widely—days, weeks, even months—based on the disease.
visual_description: Escalation visual: This can vary widely—days, weeks, even months—based on the disease.
ai_image_prompt: Escalation visual: This can vary widely—days, weeks, even months—based on the disease.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Escalation visual: This can vary widely—days, weeks, even months—based on the disease.

narration: For example, the flu can show symptoms in just one to four days, while COVID-19 might take up to 14 days.
visual_description: Evidence visual: For example, the flu can show symptoms in just one to four days, while COVID-19 might take up to 14 days.
ai_image_prompt: Evidence visual: For example, the flu can show symptoms in just one to four days, while COVID-19 might take up to 14 days.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Evidence visual: For example, the flu can show symptoms in just one to four days, while COVID-19 might take up to 14 days.

narration: Understanding this helps in early detection and containment of diseases.
